title: We're Not Very Worried About Joby Aviation's Cash Burn Rate
text: Simply Wall St's analysis suggests investors shouldn't be overly concerned about Joby Aviation's cash burn situation. The company demonstrates a reasonable financial position despite ongoing losses. As of March 2024, Joby Aviation held $924 million in cash with no debt. The company burned through $371 million annually, resulting in approximately 2.5 years of cash runway. This timeframe provides the company sufficient time to develop its business model. The analysis notes that the company is ratcheting up investment in the business with cash burn increasing 21 percent year-over-year. This acceleration could potentially shorten the company's runway. Joby Aviation has begun generating operational revenue but only produced $1.1 million last year—minimal relative to its burn rate. The company's cash burn represents about 10 percent of its $3.5 billion market capitalization. The article concludes analysts are not overly concerned about the company's cash burn, citing adequate runway and reasonable access to capital through equity offerings. However, the piece notes shareholders should monitor how cash burn develops.
